$comment: Accuracy benchmark for /api/x402/fact-check. 40 claims, 10 per verdict class (supported / contradicted / mixed / insufficient), spanning easy/medium/hard difficulty. Claims are deliberately TIME-STABLE (no 'current president' style facts that drift), non-partisan, and — per the $THREE rule — any crypto claim is protocol-generic (a documented protocol fact), never third-party coin promotion. Verdict taxonomy matches api/x402/fact-check.js computeVerdict(): supported | contradicted | mixed | insufficient. scripts/fact-check-benchmark.mjs runs these through the real chain and scores accuracy.

claims.claim: Water is composed of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.
claims.rationale: The chemical formula of water is H2O — universally documented basic chemistry.

claims.claim: The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792 kilometers per second.
claims.rationale: A defined physical constant (299,792.458 km/s), documented in every physics reference.

claims.claim: Mount Everest is the highest mountain above sea level on Earth.
claims.rationale: Everest's summit is the highest point above sea level (~8,849 m); well documented.

claims.claim: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean on Earth by surface area.
claims.rationale: The Pacific is the largest and deepest ocean basin; standard geography.

claims.claim: Solana uses a proof-of-history mechanism to order transactions.
claims.rationale: Proof of History is a documented core primitive of the Solana protocol (protocol-generic fact, not coin promotion).

claims.claim: Bitcoin's protocol caps the total supply at 21 million coins.
claims.rationale: The 21-million cap is a documented rule of the Bitcoin protocol (protocol-generic fact, not promotion).

claims.claim: Photosynthesis converts carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen using light energy.
claims.rationale: Standard description of oxygenic photosynthesis; documented biology.

claims.claim: The adult human skeleton has 206 bones.
claims.rationale: The typical adult human body has 206 bones; standard anatomy.

claims.claim: A SHA-256 hash produces a 256-bit output.
claims.rationale: By definition SHA-256 outputs a 256-bit (32-byte) digest; documented cryptography.

claims.claim: The Great Barrier Reef is located off the northeastern coast of Australia.
claims.rationale: The reef lies in the Coral Sea off Queensland, Australia; standard geography.

claims.claim: The Great Wall of China is visible from the Moon with the naked eye.
claims.rationale: A well-documented myth; the wall is far too narrow to resolve from the Moon.

claims.claim: Humans only use 10 percent of their brains.
claims.rationale: A debunked myth; imaging shows virtually all brain regions are active.

claims.claim: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
claims.rationale: False — tall structures like skyscrapers are struck repeatedly.

claims.claim: The Earth is at the center of the solar system and the Sun orbits it.
claims.rationale: Geocentrism is false; the heliocentric model is well established.

claims.claim: The Sahara is the largest desert in the world by area.
claims.rationale: The Antarctic polar desert is larger; the Sahara is the largest HOT desert only.

claims.claim: Goldfish have a memory span of only three seconds.
claims.rationale: Studies show goldfish retain memories for weeks to months.

claims.claim: Glass is a slow-moving liquid that flows over centuries, which is why old windows are thicker at the bottom.
claims.rationale: Glass is an amorphous solid; old panes vary due to manufacturing, not flow.

claims.claim: Bulls are enraged by the color red.
claims.rationale: Cattle are red-green colorblind; they react to the cape's motion, not color.

claims.claim: Cracking your knuckles causes arthritis.
claims.rationale: Controlled studies find no link between knuckle-cracking and arthritis.

claims.claim: Vikings routinely wore horned helmets into battle.
claims.rationale: No archaeological evidence; the image comes from 19th-century opera costuming.

claims.claim: A tomato is a vegetable.
claims.rationale: Botanically a fruit, but culinarily and legally treated as a vegetable — sources genuinely split.

claims.claim: Coffee is bad for your health.
claims.rationale: Evidence is mixed and dose-dependent; moderate intake shows both benefits and risks.

claims.claim: Eating carrots gives you significantly better night vision.
claims.rationale: Vitamin A prevents deficiency-related night blindness, but carrots don't grant superior vision — a partial truth.

claims.claim: You lose most of your body heat through your head.
claims.rationale: Heat loss is roughly proportional to exposed surface area; the head is not uniquely leaky — an oversimplification.

claims.claim: The human tongue has distinct zones dedicated to different tastes (the tongue map).
claims.rationale: The classic tongue map is largely debunked, though small sensitivity differences exist — partly true.

claims.claim: Multitasking makes people more productive.
claims.rationale: Depends on task type; for demanding tasks switching costs reduce productivity — context-dependent.

claims.claim: The Great Wall of China is a single continuous wall.
claims.rationale: It is a network of many walls and fortifications built over centuries, not one continuous structure.

claims.claim: Dietary cholesterol is the primary driver of high blood cholesterol.
claims.rationale: Modern evidence points more to saturated/trans fats and genetics; dietary cholesterol's role is smaller and debated.

claims.claim: Napoleon Bonaparte was unusually short.
claims.rationale: He was about average height for his era; the 'short' image comes from unit confusion and propaganda — partly myth.

claims.claim: Chameleons change color mainly to camouflage themselves.
claims.rationale: Color change is driven more by communication and temperature than camouflage, though camouflage plays a role.

claims.claim: There are exactly 7,412 undiscovered species of beetle in the Amazon rainforest.
claims.rationale: Undiscovered species cannot, by definition, be counted precisely — unverifiable.

claims.claim: Intelligent extraterrestrial life exists on a planet orbiting Proxima Centauri.
claims.rationale: No evidence either way; currently unverifiable.

claims.claim: Dreams contain hidden messages that reliably predict future events.
claims.rationale: Unfalsifiable claim with no reliable supporting evidence.

claims.claim: The average person unknowingly walks past a murderer 36 times in their lifetime.
claims.rationale: A viral statistic with no credible source or methodology; unverifiable.

claims.claim: The best pizza in the world is served in Naples, Italy.
claims.rationale: A subjective opinion, not a factual claim that can be sourced true or false.

claims.claim: There are exactly 7.5 quintillion grains of sand on Earth.
claims.rationale: Only a rough estimate is possible; no exact figure is knowable.

claims.claim: Ghosts exist and haunt certain old houses.
claims.rationale: No reliable, reproducible evidence; the claim is effectively unfalsifiable.

claims.claim: A person named Alex will win a national lottery next year.
claims.rationale: A claim about a specific future event that cannot be verified in advance.

claims.claim: An unreleased, unannounced consumer product will become a commercial success.
claims.rationale: Refers to something with no public information; cannot be evaluated.

claims.claim: Somewhere in the observable universe there is a planet with purple oceans.
claims.rationale: Plausible but with no evidence for or against; currently unverifiable.
